channel.title: Posts on My New Hugo Project
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on My New Hugo Project

item.title: Reflection Blog on unit 3
item.description: Unit 3 was entitled " Reflecting on Failure." It was a bit different from other units in that the knowledge was gained directly through an activity on a Wiki project. Thus, instead of the usual classroom approach, we engaged with a real-world failure case to develop a shared resource. Therefore, the topic was more appealing and engaging. The case study assigned to our group was on the AT&T network outage. This demonstrated how a minor error in software could cause a massive failure in communication systems. It also shows how poor testing leads to significant failure. This again brought home the point that building reliable systems, fixing bugs properly, and designing technology with care should not be taken for granted, especially when a considerable number of people depend on it. Working on this case study, I realized that most technological failures result from several causes: poor assumptions, poor communication, and, as stated earlier, overconfidence. The reading from " Right Kind of Wrong: The Science of Failing Well" taught me many new things and allowed me to understand them better. It discussed how some failures are appropriate and necessary for learning. While this notion was quite attractive, it was hard to accept because failure is typically viewed as negative. I found this unit easier to comprehend because the concept of failure was better put across to me practically. Moreover, the Wiki made learning more fun and easier and changed my perspective on failure.

item.title: Reflection Blog of first two topics
item.description: We began with the First Topic named “What is Information?” and learned various things within this topic. We have begun with DIKW Hierarchy, a model which describes how facts can be turned into understanding and improved decision-making. The DIKW model is an approach to explain how raw data are converted into valuable information and knowledge and wisdom that help us make better decisions. Raw facts constitute data. Data include figures, measurements or observations. Without any interpretation, raw facts are just plain numbers. For example temperature readings taken throughout the day are pure data. With some processing raw facts become information and through that we understand what the data signify. The next step is knowledge which can be acquired through interpretation of information by means of experience, learning and understanding. Wisdom is another stage in the chain after the knowledge because wisdom utilizes knowledge to make wise decisions. Basically the DIKW model outlines the process of how we use data to extract information, create knowledge, and then make decisions based on the gained knowledge. Another key point that we learned is creating blogs for Reflection. In the end of the unit we started a new unit entitled “Nature of Information Problems” and we have learned several topics including Brightness and contrast, Geometric transformations in images, Optimization and formal analysis, Modeling and abstraction etc. The first topic covered during this unit was the representation of images and pixels. A digital image is usually defined in a computer as a two dimensional grid of pixels. Each pixel carries the color information and thepixels play an essential role in modern computer technologies. The digital images in computers can be defined using color values. For example images are often stored in computers using RGB and each color value usually ranges from 0 to 255.The following topic covered the aspects of brightness, contrast and image histograms. They help in enhancing the visibility of images. The process of brightness involves changing the lightness of an image whereas the latter focuses on increasing the difference between the light and dark areas of an image. In the final session I gained knowledge on some geometric transformations that include rotations, scaling, and reflections. The above transformations chnage the size and orientation of images while maintaining their structure. The two topics have enabled me to gain a better understanding of the basics of digital imaging creation, manipulation etc. We also did some optimizations and solved some optimization problems. Overall these two topics were new for me but I found the second one slightly more difficult due to the concepts of images and pixels. This subject required a considerable amount of reading and analysis which eventually gave me some understanding on the topic. However I believe that all other sub topics could be understood better due to some knowledge of some topics from school earlier. For example solving optimization and geometric transformation problems involved basic mathematical calculations which I had already been taught before.
